title: JMAP upload: 25 MB body fully buffered in RAM before size check
description: handle_jmap_upload in daemon/src/jmap.rs receives body as Bytes (axum buffers the entire request body in memory before the handler runs). The UPLOAD_MAX_BYTES=25MB check happens after full buffering. A caller sending a 25MB+1 body triggers 413 but has already consumed 25MB of RAM. With concurrent uploads from the same authenticated user, this can exhaust process memory. Since the daemon is loopback-only this is constrained, but the right fix is axum body size limits at the router level, not post-read rejection.
